animal housing producing Sheep, ram in United Kingdom. Additional description: Indoor cattle system.. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary for the sheep production system includes:* On-farm production of sheep, including feed grown on the farm and imported to the farm* Production of a range of crops and stubble* Interrelationships between the sheep and cropping activities* Transport between processes* Farm establishment and infrastructure (excluding construction of new infrastructure)* Farm services* Off-farm inputs, including:	+ Feed	+ Fertilizers	+ Pesticides	+ Machinery	+ Fuel	+ Electricity	+ Water	+ Seeds	+ Other inputsThe system boundary does not include:* Carbon sequestration and biogenic carbon emissions* Medicine and seeds* Carbon emissions from locally produced feeds (if the vegetation cycle is shorter than one year)* Packaging and disposal of products* Buildings and maintenance of facilities and infrastructures* Off-farm energy production* Off-farm water treatment and supply* Off-farm waste treatment and disposal

permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, heifer in United Kingdom. Additional description: High sugar pasture for sheep and cattle.. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of the beef production system includes:* The whole dairy herd, including lactating and dry cows, heifers, calves, and bulls* Non-lactating animals of the dairy herd (bulls for insemination and labor, female calves and heifers for replacement, and male calves and young bulls for sale)* Production of local feeds, including fertilizers, irrigation, pesticides, and machinery* Production of ancillary inputs such as fuels and electricity* Drinking water for the cattle* The equipment of the farms with roads, irrigation infrastructure, buildings, and tools (excluding small-scale infrastructure)* The production of concentrates and by-products, such as molasses and manure* The breeding and fattening of cattle* The sale of calves, heifers, and young bulls* The production of grass silage and pasture* The grazing of cattle on permanent pasture* The use of manure as fertilizer* The production of electricity and diesel fuel for agricultural operations* The transportation of cattle to and from the farm* The sale of beef and other products.

permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in Ireland. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the system boundary for the beef production system:* Farm inputs and transport to the farm* Manufacture and maintenance of farm machinery and milking parlour equipment* Production of mineral supplements for the livestock and mineral fertilisers* Cultivation, harvesting, grain drying and storage of harvested crops* Plastic film for silage bales* Animal husbandry and slaughter house activities* Milking parlour operations* Non-farm manure management* Operation of offices and staff accommodation* Land use change and soil carbon sequestration (excluded)Note that the system boundary does not include:* Land use change and soil carbon sequestration (as per PAS 2050)* Operation of offices and staff accommodation* Land that has been used for agriculture for more than 20 years (as per PAS 2050)
